blocks.content: December. The last day before winter break.

They walked to school together in the cold the way they had since they were eight — Daisy on the inside of the sidewalk, Kit on the street side, her breath visible in the morning air. The neighborhood looked the same it always had. It was possible that everything looked the same.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: My aunt's. For Christmas. Back before New Year's.
At the school doors she stopped and turned, backpack over one shoulder, and looked at him in the cold morning air with her cheeks pink from the walk.
She seemed to decide something. Then she seemed to decide not to.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Merry Christmas. In case I don't see you before.

She went inside. He stood on the steps for a moment after the door closed, in the cold, with the particular weight of a thing that had almost been said twice now — at the dance, and just now — and still hadn't been.
Junior year was eight months away. He kept thinking he had time.
He was starting to wonder if that was true.
